title: New Boss
weakest_section: 04:15-05:00 range with pencil fight and basic interactions

jokes.text: Michael showing blurry photo claiming it's Johnny Depp in his condo complex
jokes.setup: Michael excitedly shows photo of supposed celebrity
jokes.punchline: It's clearly just some random guy in t-shirt and jeans
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - his desperation to connect with celebrities drives him to see what isn't there

jokes.text: I read in People magazine that he was looking for a two-bedroom condo in Scranton
jokes.setup: Michael trying to justify his Johnny Depp sighting
jokes.punchline: The absurd idea that a mega-celebrity would want a condo in Scranton
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - Michael creates elaborate backstory for his delusion

jokes.text: Well, you remember my idea for the fourth Pirates movie. Sure. That they should do one.
jokes.setup: Michael acts like he has creative input on Pirates franchise
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan revelation that Michael's 'idea' is just 'make another movie'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deadpan - deflates Michael's grandiosity with surgical precision

jokes.text: Jim calling Michael's pirate impression 'Cap'n Crunch' instead of Captain Jack Sparrow
jokes.setup: Michael doing pirate voice expecting recognition
jokes.punchline: Jim deliberately misidentifies it as breakfast cereal mascot
jokes.explanation: Great Jim prank - undermines Michael's dramatic moment with perfect misdirection

jokes.text: You know, it's not easy getting excited about stuff
jokes.setup: Michael frustrated after Jim's mockery
jokes.punchline: Accidentally profound statement about his desperate need for excitement
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly touching moment - reveals Michael's underlying sadness beneath the delusion

jokes.text: It also explains why the name on his mailbox was M. Schulman
jokes.setup: Michael still defending his Johnny Depp theory
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate explanation for obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Michael doubles down on delusion rather than admit error

jokes.text: M. Night Schulman?
jokes.setup: Michael's M. Schulman explanation
jokes.punchline: Jim connects it to M. Night Shyamalan for no logical reason
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but feels slightly forced - Jim's humor works better when more natural

jokes.text: First up, the Lost and Found has gone missing. It itself is lost
jokes.setup: Michael announcing office updates
jokes.punchline: Meta joke about the Lost and Found being lost
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels like a throwaway line - not particularly character-specific

jokes.text: But will be... Damn it! No, no, no. Meeting's not over.
jokes.setup: Sales staff getting up to leave when no sales topics mentioned
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate attempt to maintain authority as everyone ignores him
jokes.explanation: Shows power dynamic shift - Michael losing control as sales team asserts dominance

jokes.text: You couldn't handle my undivided attention
jokes.setup: Michael asking for Dwight's undivided attention
jokes.punchline: Dwight's arrogant dismissal of his former idol
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Dwight's new arrogance completely inverts his usual dynamic with Michael

jokes.text: make friends first, make sales second, make love third, in no particular order
jokes.setup: Michael explaining old office philosophy
jokes.punchline: Adding 'make love' then claiming no particular order
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - inappropriate addition followed by logical contradiction. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: All right, Dwight out! Salesman is King. As the best salesman, I am King of Kings
jokes.setup: Dwight declaring his importance as salesman
jokes.punchline: Escalates to comparing himself to Jesus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight escalation - his grandiosity knows no bounds, leading to blasphemous comparison

jokes.text: Oh, you say Jesus is King of Kings? Well, what does that say to you about how I think of myself?
jokes.setup: Dwight's King of Kings declaration
jokes.punchline: Dwight essentially claims to be Jesus-level
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Dwight doesn't back down from Jesus comparison, doubles down on it

jokes.text: Honey, if I don't have time to answer an email, I definitely don't have time to walk over to your desk
jokes.setup: Pam complaining Jim won't answer emails
jokes.punchline: Jim's backwards logic about time management
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about modern workplace communication, though not deeply character-specific

jokes.text: Stop sexting Pam
jokes.setup: Jim writing something while Michael tries to congratulate him
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate assumption about what Jim's doing
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate comment - brief and cringe-worthy but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Great. Teddy, nice. Let me just... Let me write that down real quick. Pencil. Give me a pencil.
jokes.setup: Michael interrupting Jim's call to demand pencil
jokes.punchline: Michael's petty power play during Jim's important call
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's pettiness but not particularly clever - more cringe than funny

jokes.text: The sales department smashed my sandwich. Yes. All of them. Together. It's a conspiracy.
jokes.setup: Michael's lunch got ruined
jokes.punchline: Michael creates elaborate conspiracy theory about coordinated sandwich attack
jokes.explanation: Great Michael paranoia - escalates minor incident to elaborate conspiracy theory

jokes.text: That's what she said. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Toby says 'You need to get back on top'
jokes.punchline: Michael's automatic 'That's what she said' with Toby awkwardly agreeing
jokes.explanation: Standard 'that's what she said' - predictable setup, saved only by Toby's awkward agreement

jokes.text: You know what I'm thinking? I'm thinking that I have something that you want
jokes.setup: Sales team demanding leads
jokes.punchline: Michael realizes he has leverage and his tone completely changes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - shows Michael's petty vindictiveness when he gains any power

jokes.text: I am sick of your uppity attitude, Jim
jokes.setup: Michael listing complaints about sales team
jokes.punchline: Calls Jim 'uppity' - completely wrong characterization
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Michael's complete misreading of Jim's personality

jokes.text: Okay, just imagine that instead of going to jail for murdering someone, you got an ice cream cone. If that were the case, then in the summertime, everyone would go around killing people for the pleasure of an ice cream cone
jokes.setup: Michael trying to explain incentive theory
jokes.punchline: Bizarre analogy connecting murder to ice cream and seasonal killing sprees
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - starts with reasonable point but spirals into absurd seasonal murder theory. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, if that is what I am required to do, I will do exactly that. Exactly that.
jokes.setup: Gabe ordering Michael to distribute leads
jokes.punchline: Michael's ominous repetition hints at malicious compliance
jokes.explanation: Good setup for coming malicious compliance - Michael's tone suggests brewing revenge

jokes.text: Gabe has instructed me to hand out the leads, so I am going to give the leads to King Creed. And to King Meredith! And to King Angela. Because today we are all kings and queens
jokes.setup: Michael announcing lead distribution
jokes.punchline: Gives leads to non-salespeople, declares everyone royalty
jokes.explanation: Perfect malicious compliance - Michael technically follows orders while completely subverting them

jokes.text: These aren't leads. What are they? Oh, right. Those are clues, and within each clue is a lesson
jokes.setup: Sales team expecting actual leads
jokes.punchline: Michael reveals elaborate scavenger hunt instead
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Michael's revenge - turns business necessity into teaching moment

jokes.text: I'm trying to make your kids respect you, because a father needs to respect his boss, and kids don't respect the father who doesn't respect the boss. Do you understand that line of logic? I don't even think you understand it. I do understand it.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his convoluted teaching method
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about respect that makes no sense, then claims to understand it
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael pseudo-logic - creates meaningless chain of reasoning then insists he understands it

jokes.text: How the tables have turned!
jokes.setup: Michael feeling powerful with leads situation
jokes.punchline: Michael's triumphant declaration of reversed power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael catchphrase - not particularly clever but fits character perfectly

jokes.text: It's gonna cost you some clerical work. What are these for? It doesn't matter. Fill them out. All of them. And when you're done, you can watch me shred them
jokes.setup: Michael offering leads to Stanley
jokes.punchline: Makes Stanley do pointless busywork just to watch it destroyed
jokes.explanation: Good petty revenge - shows Michael's vindictive side when he has power

jokes.text: I want to watch The Kardashians, okay? I don't want to watch Bored To Death. It's mindless! Kardashians is a good show. It is... Thank you, Stanley!
jokes.setup: Michael and Stanley arguing about TV shows while filling out forms
jokes.punchline: Michael calls Kardashians 'good' after calling other show 'mindless'
jokes.explanation: Mild irony about Michael's TV taste but not particularly sharp or memorable

jokes.text: I am currently reading incoherent riddles on blue index cards to find vital information that Michael has hidden all over the office. How are you? Nothing but vomit and diapers over here. Oh, my God. I couldn't envy you more.
jokes.setup: Jim complaining about office riddles to Pam with new baby
jokes.punchline: Jim envies Pam's baby vomit and diapers over office situation
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between work and home life - shows how absurd the office has become

jokes.text: When arrogant salesmen are mean to my face, a certain manager will go to his moppy place. He means his mopey place. It's under that street lamp that he thinks was in Casablanca
jokes.setup: Jim reading Michael's riddle with spelling error
jokes.punchline: Pam corrects 'moppy' to 'mopey' and knows exactly where Michael goes to sulk
jokes.explanation: Great character knowledge - Pam instantly understands Michael's psychology and movie delusions

jokes.text: Today I turned an office crisis into a teachable moment. A lesser manager would have screwed this day up royally
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on his handling of the situation
jokes.punchline: Michael sees chaos he created as masterful management
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - completely misreads his destructive behavior as leadership

jokes.text: Hey. I guess you probably won't give me your leads since I'm a jerk salesman. Yeah. I basically wish you were dead
jokes.setup: Jim trying reverse psychology on Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact death wish response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Michael's casual death wish is both shocking and perfectly in character

jokes.text: Colder. Warmer. Colder. Colder. Warmer. Warmer. Warmer! Hot! Hotter. Burning hot! Lower. Are you... Lower. Are you sure? Lower.
jokes.setup: Michael directing Jim in hot/cold game
jokes.punchline: Directions lead Jim to crouch awkwardly low, possibly suggestive position
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment - works better visually than in transcript, relies on implication

jokes.text: The treasure you seek is in the parking lot under the first president? Lincoln. The prankster is getting pranked
jokes.setup: Dwight solving Michael's riddle
jokes.punchline: Dwight confidently declares Lincoln first president while claiming to prank Michael
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - his confidence despite being wrong about first president is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You are never going to find them. Really? I'm going to enjoy this. Give me the leads! Where are my leads? Give me the leads! I'm still enjoying it! Where are they? They're in the trash
jokes.setup: Kevin claiming Dwight won't find leads
jokes.punchline: Kevin calmly enjoying Dwight's increasing desperation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kevin's rare moment of power and enjoyment over Dwight's frustration

jokes.text: All right, Meredith. Take off your dress. Okey-dokey. No, dear God, no. It's in the trashcan. In the kitchen. It's coming off either way
jokes.setup: Dwight thinks 'trash' means Meredith (calling her trashy)
jokes.punchline: Meredith immediately starts undressing, horrifying everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith moment - her eagerness to undress is both shocking and completely in character

jokes.text: I emptied it in the dumpster. It had Toby's baba ghanoush. If we don't patronize the only Syrian restaurant in town, there'll be nothing left but pan pizzas and, you know, make-your-own-salads
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining why he emptied trash
jokes.punchline: Oscar's pretentious concern for Syrian restaurant diversity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar - turns mundane trash disposal into lecture about cultural food diversity

jokes.text: What day is today? Tonight is Ghost Whisperer, so Friday. Oh, my God. Oh, my God! Oh, my God! No, no, no!
jokes.setup: Michael realizing what day it is
jokes.punchline: Kevin uses TV schedule to determine day, Michael realizes trash pickup happened
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kevin's TV-based calendar system and Michael's dawning horror

jokes.text: Hey, hey! Wait! Wait! Okay, you know what? Let's just go to the dump, start looking. Ryan, come on. Shotgun in my car
jokes.setup: Michael chasing garbage truck
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate plan to search dump, calling shotgun in his own car
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's desperation - the 'shotgun in my car' detail adds absurdity to crisis

jokes.text: You were all successories
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say 'accessories' to a crime
jokes.punchline: Michael creates malapropism combining 'accessories' and 'Successories' motivational posters
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael malapropism - perfectly combines his love of motivational posters with legal terminology

jokes.text: This place has gone to hell
jokes.setup: Michael reflecting on office situation at dump
jokes.punchline: Michael blames office while literally standing in garbage dump he created situation for
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Michael lamenting office decline while standing in literal dump of his creation

jokes.text: You know, Dwight, there was a time you'd be pinching yourself to have the opportunity to look through a dump with me. Yeah, well, the acorn becomes the oak. Yeah. Well, sometimes the acorn just stays an acorn. If you don't believe me, look in my gutters
jokes.setup: Michael nostalgic about old relationship with Dwight
jokes.punchline: Michael's surprisingly wise metaphor about failed potential, with oddly specific gutter reference
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly profound Michael moment - wise metaphor about potential, undermined by specific gutter detail

jokes.text: I have new baby pictures. Jim. Don't use your cute baby to make us like you. She's wearing a Onesie. Stop it.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to show baby pictures during office tension
jokes.punchline: Michael recognizes and resists emotional manipulation, but is clearly affected
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Michael's awareness of manipulation but inability to resist baby cuteness

jokes.text: If we act nice now, then we're rewarding them for treating us poorly. Didn't we kind of start it? Mmm. I think you're remembering that wrong
jokes.setup: Pam questioning if they started the conflict
jokes.punchline: Jim's selective memory revision to maintain moral high ground
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamics - shows Jim's human tendency to rewrite history when convenient

jokes.text: I mean, a lot of their work can be done from India
jokes.setup: Andy talking about office staff during conflict
jokes.punchline: Casual threat of outsourcing jobs shows how sales success has changed Andy
jokes.explanation: Dark character development - shows how success has made Andy callous about colleagues' livelihoods

jokes.text: You've changed, man. Oh, why, 'cause I have a shot at a 100K commission? Since when do you care about money? When I first met you, you were a wide-eyed innocent. Hey, there is nothing I can do about my wide-set eyes
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Dwight's personality change
jokes.punchline: Dwight mishears 'wide-eyed' as 'wide-set' and defends his eye spacing
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Dwight's literal interpretation of metaphorical language creates perfect comedic misunderstanding

jokes.text: And I had an offer from Ivan Schotsky. The Ivan Schotsky. If I had assistant managed him... Assistant to the managed him. Oh, that's low! I would be number two right now at a Home Depot!
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming he had other job opportunities
jokes.punchline: Michael corrects his grammar, Dwight's ultimate career goal is Home Depot management
jokes.explanation: Great callback to running gag plus perfect Dwight ambition - Home Depot management as pinnacle of success

jokes.text: I hitched my wagon to a horse with no legs
jokes.setup: Dwight expressing frustration with following Michael
jokes.punchline: Dwight's mixed metaphor about backing the wrong horse
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor - vivid image of Michael as completely useless horse perfectly captures Dwight's frustration

jokes.text: I should have been out at bars finding my soul mate, finding my wife, making babies. Nice babies you're making with the floozies at the bars! That's my wife you're talking about, man! Your made-up wife who doesn't exist?
jokes.setup: Dwight lamenting missed romantic opportunities
jokes.punchline: Michael insults hypothetical bar wife, Dwight defends her, Michael points out she doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Dwight gets emotionally invested in defending his imaginary wife from insults

jokes.text: You watch it! If I'd wanted to hit you, I would have hit you
jokes.setup: Physical confrontation building between Michael and Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight's threatening non-threat about violence
jokes.explanation: Standard tough-guy posturing - fits Dwight's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Stop it! Give me that. That's my spool!
jokes.setup: Michael and Dwight fighting at dump
jokes.punchline: Their serious fight is over a garbage spool
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - serious emotional fight reduced to squabble over literal garbage

jokes.text: Pam texted back saying we could give them all iPods. If they don't have an iPod by now, they really don't want one
jokes.setup: Sales team trying to find peace offering
jokes.punchline: Andy's logic that iPods are outdated/unwanted by now
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about technology obsolescence - feels authentic to 2009 timeframe

jokes.text: Erin just texted me back. 'People love shells from faraway beaches.'
jokes.setup: Andy asking Erin for gift advice
jokes.punchline: Erin's completely impractical and random suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment - her naive, completely unhelpful advice delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Amazing, isn't it? No other animal on Earth could do this. Maybe beavers. But not like this
jokes.setup: Michael philosophical about human achievement at dump
jokes.punchline: Michael finds profound meaning in garbage dumps, considers beaver comparison
jokes.explanation: Great Michael moment - finds wonder in mundane things, second-guesses himself about beavers

jokes.text: So, how about, guys, 1% commission a month instead of 2%? What do you think... No. No, we agreed 2% for the quarter
jokes.setup: Michael trying to negotiate at dump
jokes.punchline: Sales team immediately shuts down negotiation attempt
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's desperation but not particularly funny - more plot advancement than comedy

jokes.text: Way to go, guys. This... This was an integrity move. Yeah. When we walked in here, we were all prepared to tell you to go to hell. Do you have any pastries without fruit? Yes, we do. Eclairs
jokes.setup: Stanley praising sales team's peace offering
jokes.punchline: Immediately transitions to demanding specific pastries
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - forgives instantly when food is involved, immediately makes demands

jokes.text: Well, you better be happy, taking 2% of our... 2% milk! What I forgot for the coffee. Yeah, treats, Stanley. They've accepted our simple offer of treats only, nothing more
jokes.setup: Jim almost revealing the commission deal
jokes.punchline: Covers by pretending he meant milk percentage for coffee
jokes.explanation: Excellent save - Jim's quick thinking to cover near-slip with mundane milk reference

jokes.text: This? Why would somebody throw that out? Waste. Hey. You know who'd like this? Phyllis. Purple much? Yeah, she does. She loves purple
jokes.setup: Michael finding purple item in dump
jokes.punchline: Michael's certainty about Phyllis's color preferences, Dwight's confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good character knowledge moment - shows how well they know Phyllis's aesthetic preferences

jokes.text: You know what would be a great picture here? Just this whole dump, and in the middle, one flower. Mmm-hmm. That's it. Wow. And the caption would read... 'Hope grows.' 'In a dump.'
jokes.setup: Michael getting artistic inspiration at dump
jokes.punchline: Michael's cliché motivational poster concept with unnecessarily literal caption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael - finds inspiration in dump, creates terrible motivational poster concept with redundant caption

jokes.text: Is the good news that you found our leads? No. Better. We have an awesome beanbag chair that's perfect for the break room. I'm never gonna sit on that disgusting seat. Yeah, damn right you're not, 'cause it's for me and Michael only!
jokes.setup: Stanley asking about leads, Michael reveals dump treasure
jokes.punchline: Michael calls filthy dump chair 'awesome,' Dwight excludes Stanley from using it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Michael's misplaced enthusiasm, Dwight's petty exclusion of Stanley

jokes.text: I have warm blood. Oh, wow. Thank you. You're the nicest person I've ever met
jokes.setup: Michael and Dwight sharing moment in cold
jokes.punchline: Dwight stating basic biological fact, Michael treating it as profound kindness
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Michael's desperation for connection makes him grateful for basic human warmth

tmdb_overview: Michael has big plans for his 15th anniversary party, but a new VP has different ideas.
